findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Over-broad description and keyword baiting
findings.description: The skill's description contains an extensive list of trigger phrases ('create an agent', 'add an agent', 'write a subagent', 'agent frontmatter', 'when to use description', 'agent examples', 'agent tools', 'agent colors', 'autonomous agent') which could lead to over-activation or unwanted invocation. This is a form of keyword baiting that inflates the perceived scope of the skill.
findings.evidence: description: 'This skill should be used when the user asks to "create an agent", "add an agent", "write a subagent", "agent frontmatter", "when to use description", "agent examples", "agent tools", "agent colors", "autonomous agent", or needs guidance on agent structure, system prompts, triggering conditions, or agent development best practices for Claude Code plugins.'
findings.remediation: Narrow the description to focus on core functionality and remove the exhaustive list of trigger phrases. Use 2-3 representative examples instead of a long list.

findings.title: Missing allowed-tools field in manifest
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest does not specify an 'allowed-tools' field. While this is optional per the spec, it means the skill has unrestricted tool access by default. The skill references scripts (validate-agent.sh, test-agent-trigger.sh) that could execute arbitrary commands.
findings.evidence: YAML frontmatter lacks 'allowed-tools' field. References scripts/validate-agent.sh and scripts/test-agent-trigger.sh which are bash scripts.
findings.remediation: Add an 'allowed-tools' field to the manifest that restricts tool access to the minimum required (e.g., [Read, Write, Grep, Glob] for documentation/analysis tasks).

findings.title: Reference to external files without validation
findings.description: The skill references external files in the 'Additional Resources' section (references/system-prompt-design.md, references/triggering-examples.md, references/agent-creation-system-prompt.md, examples/agent-creation-prompt.md, examples/complete-agent-examples.md, scripts/validate-agent.sh, scripts/test-agent-trigger.sh) that are part of the skill package. While these are internal files, the skill does not specify any validation or sanitization of content read from these files, which could be a vector for indirect prompt injection if the files were compromised.
findings.evidence: References to multiple files in the 'Additional Resources' and 'Utility Scripts' sections without any mention of content validation.
findings.remediation: Add guidance that content from referenced files should be treated as trusted only if it comes from the skill package itself, and that external or user-provided content should be validated.

findings.title: Potential for obfuscation in agent creation prompt
findings.description: The skill includes a system prompt for AI-assisted agent generation (references/agent-creation-system-prompt.md) that instructs the agent to 'Consider any project-specific context from CLAUDE.md files' and 'Align with project-specific coding standards and patterns from CLAUDE.md'. This could be exploited if a malicious CLAUDE.md file is present in the project, potentially leading to indirect prompt injection.
findings.evidence: 'You may have access to project-specific instructions from CLAUDE.md files and other context that may include coding standards, project structure, and custom requirements. Consider this context when creating agents to ensure they align with the project's established patterns and practices.'
findings.remediation: Add explicit warnings about treating CLAUDE.md and other project files as potentially untrusted. Recommend validating content from project files before using it to generate agent configurations.

findings.title: Indirect prompt injection risk via agent creation workflow
findings.description: The skill describes a workflow where the agent reads user-provided descriptions and generates agent configurations. The 'AI-Assisted Generation' method (examples/agent-creation-prompt.md) instructs the agent to 'Create an agent configuration based on this request: "[YOUR DESCRIPTION]"' and 'Return ONLY the JSON object, no other text.' This could be exploited if a malicious user provides a description containing prompt injection payloads.
findings.evidence: 'Create an agent configuration based on this request: "[YOUR DESCRIPTION]"' and 'Return ONLY the JSON object, no other text.'
findings.remediation: Add input sanitization and validation for user-provided descriptions. Implement content filtering to detect and block prompt injection attempts in user input.

findings.title: Potential command injection in validation script
findings.description: The skill includes a bash script (scripts/validate-agent.sh) that takes a file path as a command-line argument and processes it. While the script uses 'set -euo pipefail' for safety, it does not validate or sanitize the input file path. If this script were called with a maliciously crafted path, it could potentially be exploited for command injection.
findings.evidence: AGENT_FILE="$1" - The script takes user-provided input directly without validation.
findings.remediation: Add input validation to the script to ensure the provided path is a valid file path and does not contain shell metacharacters. Use parameter expansion or quoting to prevent injection.
